title: Custom Authorities
description: View, create, update and delete your account's custom authorities
noneDescription: You have not created any custom authorities for this account yet.
createDialogDescription: Grant a specific operation permission to another account or key under conditions you define.
deleteDialogDescription: This will permanently delete the custom authority {{id}}. This action cannot be undone.
enabledHint: Whether the custom authority is active.
operationTypeHint: The operation this authority grants permission for.
operationTypeLockedHint: The operation type cannot be changed after creation.
authHint: Weight threshold plus account, key and address auths.
searchAccountDescription: Search for the account to authorize.
restrictionsHint: Conditions the operation must satisfy. Add rows below, or pick a template.
restrictionsToRemoveHint: Select existing restrictions to remove from this authority.
noExistingRestrictions: This authority has no restrictions to remove.
invalidAuthJson: The authority field contains invalid JSON.
invalidRestrictionsJson: The restrictions field contains invalid JSON.
vErrNotArray: Restrictions must be a JSON array.
vErrMemberIndex: Restriction {{path}} has an invalid member_index (expected a non-negative integer).
vErrArgumentShape: Restriction {{path}} argument must be a [type, value] pair.
vErrArgTag: Restriction {{path}} has an invalid argument type tag.
vErrExtensions: Restriction {{path}} extensions must be an array.
vErrOrShape: Restriction {{path}} logical_or argument must be a list of restriction lists.
voidValueHint: No value — the member must be left unset (none).
noFieldsForOp: This operation has no predefined fields. Use Advanced (JSON) to add restrictions.
coverageIntro: Any operation member left unrestricted can be set freely by the authorized party.
warnUncoveredMandatory: Unrestricted required members: {{fields}}. The authorized party can set these to any value.
warnUnpinnedOptional: Optional members not pinned to none: {{fields}}. Pin them to none to prevent the authorized party from setting them.
warnExtensions: This operation has an extensions member. It cannot be forced to none as a whole; each extension sub-member must be individually restricted.
warnBroadOp: This is a broad, flexible operation. Restrict it tightly — an unrestricted authority here is very powerful.
